mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production over the trailing three months signals expanding manufacturing activity, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. This trend signal is always active and requires no threshold condition.
failure_condition: Rank IC of trailing 3-month change in INDPRO on 20-day forward returns is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate by more than 0.2 percentage points over three months signals weakening labor market conditions, reducing consumer spending power and corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points over a 3-month period
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the unemployment trigger fires, violating the negative directional prediction.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4% and the CPI inflation index is above 200, the central bank is actively tightening monetary policy to combat elevated inflation. This dual condition compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced liquidity, producing negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered instances, contradicting the expected negative direction.

mechanism_narrative: During the early to mid-cycle recovery phase, rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) reflects improving labor market conditions and rising aggregate income, which directly supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: In the regime identified by falling UNRATE and stable consumer confidence, positive PAYEMS changes do not produce significantly positive average 60-day returns (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in retail sales growth (RSAFS) signals weakening consumer demand, which rapidly reduces near-term corporate revenue expectations. This demand-side shock typically results in negative S&P 500 returns within the next five trading days as markets reprice growth assumptions.
failure_condition: In more than 55% of trigger events, 5-day forward returns are positive instead of negative as predicted.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent rise in the CPI inflation index erodes real purchasing power and forces the central bank to consider tighter policy, which increases discount rates on future equity cash flows. This inflation trend signal is always active and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC of trailing 12-month change in CPIAUCSL on 60-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts are above 2,000 thousand units and the federal funds rate is below 4.5%, accommodative monetary policy is fueling strong housing demand. This joint condition signals robust economic momentum and optimistic consumer sentiment, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with strong housing demand
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of instances when both conditions are met, contradicting the expected positive outcome.

mechanism_narrative: In the late-cycle expansion phase, when GDP growth is above potential and resource utilization is high, further increases in real GDP (GDPC1) signal overheating and impending recession. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon as markets anticipate a peak.
failure_condition: When the economy is identified as late-cycle (GDP above trend, capacity utilization high), positive GDP surprises are followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases, violating the negative directional prediction.
